name: PERCEIVE
description: PERCEIVE (Psychophysics-driven Elicitation for Routing Cost-Efficiency In Vision-Language Evaluation) is a VLM routing benchmark of 4,801 document-image QA samples across 16 public datasets and 6 task families. Each sample is annotated with psychophysical complexity scores (Visual Dependency Score, Reasoning Depth Score, Spatial Extent Score on an ordinal 1-4 scale), stratified into Easy, Medium, and Hard tiers via Bayesian threshold estimation, and evaluated across 7 commercial VLMs at up to 4 reasoning-budget levels (24 valid configurations). Routing labels indicating the cheapest-correct model-budget configuration per query are derived via a QUEST-style adaptive cascade achieving 60.7% cost reduction with 100% ground-truth label agreement. The benchmark includes a 1,500-sample exhaustively-evaluated anchor set (split=anchor), a 750-sample held-out validation set (split=validation), and 2,551 test queries (split=test). Supports incremental model-pool extension via Inductive Matrix Completion.
rai:dataCollection: Samples are derived from 16 publicly available document-image datasets spanning six task families. No new images were collected; all source images were obtained through their respective official distribution channels (HuggingFace Hub or original dataset repositories). A stratified submodular facility-location sampler selected 4,801 samples from a candidate pool of 15,482, targeting a 25/50/25 Easy/Medium/Hard tier distribution. Psychophysical complexity annotations (VDS, RDS, SES) were elicited automatically from a two-model probe oracle (GPT-5.4-mini and Gemini-2.5-Flash-Lite) using a structured prompt (Appendix C of the paper). No human annotators were employed for complexity scoring.
rai:dataAnnotationProtocol: Ground-truth routing labels were derived via a QUEST-style adaptive cascade (Appendix D). Two probe models (GPT-5.4-mini, Gemini-2.5-Flash-Lite) independently estimate VDS/RDS/SES scores; a Bayesian posterior over psychophysical thresholds assigns each sample to a difficulty tier. Model-budget evaluations use a three-stage arbiter: rule-based scorers (ANLS, exact match, IoU, F1), a Mistral Small 2603 neural judge on borderline outputs, and a Mistral Large 2512 oracle arbiter on the ~11.8% of rule-neural disagreements. Routing labels were validated against the 1,244-sample routable anchor subset, achieving 100% per-query routing-label agreement and an 8.2% per-check Dominance Violation Rate (95% CI 8.0%-8.3%) over 171,392 candidate-cheaper checks.
rai:dataBiases: Source datasets skew toward English-language Western business documents (invoices, receipts, forms) and academic publications. Chart and table datasets reflect US/European data conventions. These biases are inherited from source datasets and documented here to guide appropriate use.
rai:dataLimitations: Source coverage: 16 document-image datasets spanning commercial documents, scientific publications, and web images; natural scene images and medical imaging are not represented. Language: English only. Model pool: 7 commercial VLMs as of early 2026; IMC generalises to new models at K=25 calibration anchors (AUC 0.833-0.873) and to held-out queries from parse features alone (AUC 0.876) within known document types, but degrades on novel document types (AUC 0.60 cross-domain holdout). Unroutable ceiling: 18.5% of samples across the full benchmark are unanswerable by any configuration in the pool (20.7% on the held-out validation split; 17.6% on the anchor split). Probe stability: tier assignments are stable (>90%) under single-model dropout and 10% perturbation.
rai:personalSensitiveInformation: No personally identifiable information. Source datasets contain document images from public domain sources. PERCEIVE adds no PII beyond what is present in the original datasets.
rai:dataSocialImpact: Primary benefit: reduces inference costs for document-understanding applications by routing queries to the cheapest sufficient VLM. The benchmark enables reproducible evaluation of routing policies, reducing redundant API expenditure. The model pool covers only commercial APIs; open-source model routing is not directly supported, which may limit applicability in cost-sensitive or privacy-constrained settings. No high-risk social applications (healthcare decisions, legal judgments, surveillance) are intended or validated.
rai:dataUseCases: Intended for: (1) benchmarking VLM routing and cost-efficiency strategies on document-understanding tasks; (2) evaluating psychophysics-driven complexity metrics for vision-language tasks; (3) studying the relationship between task complexity and model capability across document types; (4) training and validating routing classifiers. Not intended for: training generative models, evaluating general-purpose VLM capability (no open-ended generation tasks), or human subject research.
prov:wasGeneratedBy: PERCEIVE benchmark construction pipeline: (1) submodular facility-location sampling from 16 source datasets; (2) psychophysical complexity annotation via two-model probe oracle (GPT-5.4-mini, Gemini-2.5-Flash-Lite) using QUEST-style adaptive cascade; (3) exhaustive model-budget evaluation across 24 valid configurations (7 VLMs × up to 4 reasoning budgets) for the 1,500-sample anchor set; (4) QUEST-style cascade label propagation to remaining 3,301 samples; (5) routing label derivation as cheapest-correct configuration per sample.

distribution.name: benchmark_5000.jsonl
distribution.description: 4,801 document-image QA samples with VDS/RDS/SES complexity annotations, difficulty tier (1-3), visual-element flags, split membership (anchor/validation/test), and evaluation metadata.

distribution.name: routing_labels.jsonl
distribution.description: 4,801 routing labels: cheapest-correct model-budget configuration per sample, derived via QUEST-style adaptive cascade. Includes is_routable flag and eval_signal.

distribution.name: final_eval_correct.jsonl
distribution.description: 77,719 per-(sample, model, budget) correctness judgments covering the anchor and validation sets across all 28 configurations plus external validation models.

distribution.name: oracle_verdicts.jsonl
distribution.description: 7,974 oracle arbiter decisions for ambiguous evaluation cases where rule-based scorers and neural judge disagreed.

distribution.name: all_models_judgments_v2.jsonl
distribution.description: 78,308 raw neural judge outputs for (sample, model, budget) pairs including rule-based scorer decisions and Mistral-Large neural fallback responses.

distribution.name: probe_results.jsonl
distribution.description: Probe oracle elicitation outputs: VDS, RDS, SES scores from each of the three probe models (GPT-4o, Claude-3.5-Sonnet, Gemini-1.5-Pro) for all 4,801 samples.

recordSet.name: benchmark_records
recordSet.description: Records from benchmark_5000.jsonl. Each record is one document-image QA sample with psychophysical complexity annotations and split assignment.

field.name: sample_id
field.description: Unique identifier combining source dataset name and index.

field.name: split
field.description: Dataset split: anchor (1,500 exhaustively-evaluated samples), validation (750 held-out), or test (2,551 cascade-labeled queries).

field.name: source_dataset
field.description: Source dataset name. One of: DocVQA, SlideVQA, TextVQA, ChartQA, SROIE, InfographicVQA, PubLayNet, RVL-CDIP, MP-DocVQA, ST-VQA, VisualMRC, FUNSD, HierText, WTQ, CORD, TabFact.

field.name: task_type
field.description: Task family: T1 (document classification), T2 (structured field extraction), T3 (layout and spatial reasoning), T4 (semantic and compositional QA), T5 (multi-page QA), T6 (element localization).

field.name: query
field.description: Natural-language question about the document image.

field.name: gt_answer
field.description: Primary ground-truth answer, inherited from source dataset.

field.name: correctness_metric
field.description: Evaluation metric: anls, exact_match, field_f1, or iou.

field.name: tier_final
field.description: Difficulty tier: 1 (Easy), 2 (Medium), 3 (Hard). Assigned by Bayesian posterior over VDS/RDS/SES probe scores.

field.name: vds_probe_avg
field.description: Visual Dependency Score (1-4): averaged across three probe models. Measures reliance on visual features beyond OCR text.

field.name: rds_probe_avg
field.description: Reasoning Depth Score (1-4): averaged across three probe models. Measures reasoning steps required.

field.name: ses_probe_avg
field.description: Spatial Extent Score (1-4): averaged across three probe models. Measures fraction of document that must be attended.

field.name: has_table
field.description: Boolean: document image contains a structured table.

field.name: has_chart
field.description: Boolean: document image contains a chart or graph.

field.name: image_path
field.description: Relative path to document image within the source dataset archive.

recordSet.name: routing_label_records
recordSet.description: Records from routing_labels.jsonl. Each record identifies the cheapest-correct model-budget configuration per sample.

field.name: cheapest_correct_model
field.description: YAML key of cheapest model answering correctly. Null if unroutable.

field.name: cheapest_correct_budget
field.description: Budget tier of cheapest correct config: B0 (0 tokens), B1 (1,024), B2 (4,096), B3 (16,384). Null if unroutable.

field.name: is_routable
field.description: Boolean: at least one model-budget configuration answers correctly. False for the 20.7% unroutable ceiling.

field.name: split
field.description: Split membership: anchor, validation, or test.
